title: The rebuilding of Iran's ballistic arsenal challenges claims of a decisive US victory
subhead: Investigations by 'The New York Times' and 'The Washington Post' say that Iran has managed to preserve part of its missile stockpiles and launchers.

subhead: Although the UK prime minister has been fiercely criticized within Labour, none of his opponents have so far put themselves forward to replace him. The procedure to oust the leader is complex and risky.

title: 2026 World Cup: France coach Deschamps reveals squad with surprises, key players and bold offensive shift
subhead: The coach of France's national football team revealed the 26-player squad selected to compete in the World Cup, which will be held in the United States, Canada and Mexico from June 11 to July 19.

title: With generative AI, 'humankind has created, for the first time in its history, a being that mimics it in its very essence: the ability to think, to create, to decide'
subhead: Generative artificial intelligence is already having tangible effects on the labor market, including the intensification of tasks, cognitive surrender and job losses, observes Le Monde's Marie Charrel in her column.

How China is cracking down on its Christians

title: 'Zinédine Zidane maintains a political and social awareness that is deeply connected to his own story'
subhead: The sociologist Stéphane Beaud explored in an essay the football player's career, framing it within that of the descendants of Algerian immigrants. In an interview, he explains that the athlete tried to make up for the injustices suffered by his father, who remained at the bottom of the social ladder throughout his life.

title: Military researcher Céline Marangé on the Ukraine war: 'What is truly in danger is the future of European liberal democracy'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the political scientist places the conflict in Ukraine within the long history of Russian imperialism and assesses the threat that the Kremlin poses to NATO and EU countries.
